description:
WHY: bl review (commit 83a5f5a) added bare-repo support via detached worktree, but Store::discover wasn't updated. Result: every command that runs from a bare repo root (bl prime, bl ready, bl list, bl show, ...) fails with "not initialized. Run \`bl init\`" — even when the repo is fully initialized. Users can't get to bl review from the bare root at all; they have to cd into a worktree. This contradicts the bare-repo support work and is silently misleading.

REPRO: in /home/mark/dev/balls (bare repo, .balls/config.json present, state worktree healthy):
  $ bl prime --as x --json
  error: not initialized. Run \`bl init\`

ROOT CAUSE: Store::discover_git → git::git_root → \`git rev-parse --show-toplevel\`. In a bare repo this exits 128 ("this operation must be run in a work tree"). git_root returns NotARepo. discover falls back to discover_no_git, which finds .balls/config.json fine but rejects at store.rs:86 because !stealth (no .balls/local/tasks_dir override). Returns NotInitialized.

FIX: detect bare repos in discover_git (e.g. git rev-parse --is-bare-repository) and treat the bare gitdir's parent as main_root. The state-worktree path is already canonical (.balls/worktree/.balls/tasks), so once main_root is resolved, the rest works. Reuse the same bare-detection pattern bl review already uses (see src/bare_squash.rs).
title: Store::discover doesn't handle bare repos — emits misleading 'not initialized'
